title: Getting started with bouquets
text: bouquet plot (also called angular accumulation plot) converts directional dynamics time series 2-D turtle-graphics path: increase turns heading left fixed angle θ\theta. decrease turns right θ\theta. change keeps heading straight. series share origin angle, paths look alike come series similar /patterns — necessarily similar values. technique structurally related DNA walk visualisations (Gates 1986).

title: Getting started with bouquets
text: Column order defaults make call even shorter data already right column order: make_plot_bouquet(gw_long, verbose = FALSE).
code: set.seed(42) n      <- 52L weeks  <- seq(as.Date("2023-01-01"), by = "week", length.out = n) season <- sin(seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = n))  gw_long <- tibble::tibble(   week    = rep(weeks, 8L),   station = rep(paste0("Station ", LETTERS[1:8]), each = n),   region  = rep(c("North", "North", "North", "North",                   "South", "South", "South", "South"), each = n),   level_m = c(     8.5 + 0.8 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.00, 0.18)),     7.2 + 0.5 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.02, 0.22)),     9.1 + 1.1 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n, -0.01, 0.15)),     6.8 + 0.9 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.01, 0.20)),     5.4 + 1.2 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n, -0.02, 0.17)),     7.7 + 0.6 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.00, 0.21)),     8.9 + 0.7 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.01, 0.19)),     6.2 + 1.0 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n, -0.01, 0.16))   ) )  make_plot_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col   = week,   series_col = station,   value_col  = level_m,   verbose    = FALSE )

title: Getting started with bouquets
text: stem_colors (path lines) flower_colors (✿ end markers) accept four different types input.

title: Getting started with bouquets
code: make_plot_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col      = week,   series_col    = station,   value_col     = level_m,   stem_colors   = "#2d6a9f",   flower_colors = "#e07b39",   verbose       = FALSE )

title: Getting started with bouquets
text: "greens" "blossom" select curated palettes:
code: make_plot_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col      = week,   series_col    = station,   value_col     = level_m,   stem_colors   = "greens",   flower_colors = "blossom",   verbose       = FALSE )

what: 3 — Vector of hex colours (one per series)
title: Getting started with bouquets
code: make_plot_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col      = week,   series_col    = station,   value_col     = level_m,   stem_colors   = c("#c0392b", "#2980b9", "#27ae60"),   verbose       = FALSE ) #> Warning: Fewer `stem_colors` supplied than series; recycling.

title: Getting started with bouquets
text: Pass bare column name. unique value column gets distinct perceptually uniform colour (via hues::iwanthue()):
code: make_plot_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col      = week,   series_col    = station,   value_col     = level_m,   stem_colors   = region,   flower_colors = region,   verbose       = FALSE )

title: Getting started with bouquets
text: facet_by accepts column name splits plot via ggplot2::facet_wrap():
code: make_plot_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col      = week,   series_col    = station,   value_col     = level_m,   stem_colors   = region,   flower_colors = region,   facet_by      = region,   title         = "Groundwater dynamics by region",   verbose       = FALSE )

title: Getting started with bouquets
text: show_rings = TRUE adds faint concentric circles fixed radii aid distance judgements:
code: make_plot_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col   = week,   series_col = station,   value_col  = level_m,   show_rings = TRUE,   verbose    = FALSE )

title: Getting started with bouquets
text: marker_every places dot every N-th step, useful reading temporal progress:
code: make_plot_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col     = week,   series_col   = station,   value_col    = level_m,   marker_every = 13L,   # quarterly for weekly data   verbose      = FALSE )

title: Getting started with bouquets
code: make_plot_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col      = week,   series_col    = station,   value_col     = level_m,   stem_colors   = "greens",   flower_colors = "blossom",   dark_mode     = TRUE,   title         = "Groundwater — dark mode",   verbose       = FALSE )

title: Getting started with bouquets
text: launch_deg sets initial heading (90° = upward, default). ceiling_pct controls tight turns : values close 1 make turns pronounced; smaller values leave headroom loop occur.
code: make_plot_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col    = week,   series_col  = station,   value_col   = level_m,   launch_deg  = 45,   ceiling_pct = 0.60,   verbose     = FALSE )

title: Getting started with bouquets
text: cluster_bouquet() groups series similarity directional sequences appends cluster factor column. result plugs directly make_plot_bouquet(). Colour facet bouquet cluster one pipe:
code: set.seed(42) n_big  <- 52L weeks  <- seq(as.Date("2023-01-01"), by = "week", length.out = n_big) season <- sin(seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = n_big))  gw6 <- tibble::tibble(   week    = rep(weeks, 6L),   station = rep(paste0("S", 1:6), each = n_big),   level_m = c(     8.5 + 0.8 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n_big,  0.00, 0.2)),     8.3 + 0.7 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n_big,  0.01, 0.2)),     7.2 + 0.5 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n_big,  0.02, 0.2)),     7.0 + 0.6 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n_big,  0.00, 0.2)),     9.1 + 1.1 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n_big, -0.01, 0.2)),     9.3 + 1.0 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n_big, -0.02, 0.2))   ) )  clustered <- cluster_bouquet(gw6,   time_col   = week,   series_col = station,   value_col  = level_m,   verbose    = FALSE )  dplyr::distinct(clustered, station, cluster) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   station cluster #>   <chr>   <fct>   #> 1 S1      C1      #> 2 S2      C3      #> 3 S3      C2      #> 4 S4      C1      #> 5 S5      C4      #> 6 S6      C2 clustered |>   make_plot_bouquet(     time_col      = week,     series_col    = station,     value_col     = level_m,     stem_colors   = cluster,     flower_colors = cluster,     facet_by      = cluster,     title         = "Series grouped by directional dynamics",     verbose       = FALSE   )

title: Getting started with bouquets
text: Five algorithms available via method argument: distance argument ("euclidean", "correlation", "manhattan") applies "hclust" "pam". Correlation distance measures whether two series co-move regardless amplitude, often meaningful choice hydrological data:
code: cluster_bouquet(gw6,   time_col   = week,   series_col = station,   value_col  = level_m,   method     = "hclust",   distance   = "correlation",   verbose    = FALSE ) |>   dplyr::distinct(station, cluster) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   station cluster #>   <chr>   <fct>   #> 1 S1      C1      #> 2 S2      C3      #> 3 S3      C2      #> 4 S4      C1      #> 5 S5      C4      #> 6 S6      C2

title: Getting started with bouquets
text: k = "auto" (default), function fits model k=2,…,kmaxk = 2, \ldots, k_\text{max} selects k maximises: score(k)=s‾(k)×mincs‾c(k)×kresolution\text{score}(k) = \bar{s}(k) \times \min_c \bar{s}_c(k) \times k^{\text{resolution}} worst-cluster term mincs‾c(k)\min_c \bar{s}_c(k) prevents choosing k one cluster poorly defined. resolution exponent rewards finer groupings remain well-separated. Increase push toward clusters: Fix k directly already know many groups need:
code: cluster_bouquet(gw6,   time_col   = week,   series_col = station,   value_col  = level_m,   resolution = 1.5,   verbose    = FALSE ) |>   dplyr::distinct(station, cluster) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   station cluster #>   <chr>   <fct>   #> 1 S1      C1      #> 2 S2      C3      #> 3 S3      C2      #> 4 S4      C1      #> 5 S5      C4      #> 6 S6      C2 cluster_bouquet(gw6,   time_col   = week,   series_col = station,   value_col  = level_m,   k          = 2L,   verbose    = FALSE ) |>   dplyr::distinct(station, cluster) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   station cluster #>   <chr>   <fct>   #> 1 S1      C2      #> 2 S2      C1      #> 3 S3      C1      #> 4 S4      C2      #> 5 S5      C1      #> 6 S6      C1

title: Getting started with bouquets
text: coordinate columns supplied, make_plot_bouquet() attaches location map right bouquet. Points coloured match flower markers. map background drawn maps package (country outlines) mapdata (sub-national boundaries) installed. Coordinates WGS84 decimal degrees work directly. projected coordinates supply EPSG code via coord_crs:  map_width controls relative width map panel (default 0.35 = 35 % combined figure width). map automatically zoomed padded around point locations, works country region.
code: # Stations spread across Germany north to south, coast to border gw_coords <- dplyr::mutate(gw_long,   lon = rep(c(6.9, 13.4, 9.9, 12.1, 7.5, 11.2, 8.7, 14.8), each = n),   lat = rep(c(53.6, 52.5, 51.5, 48.1, 51.2, 49.8, 47.8, 50.9), each = n) )  make_plot_bouquet(gw_coords,   time_col   = week,   series_col = station,   value_col  = level_m,   lon_col    = lon,   lat_col    = lat,   map_width  = 0.35,   verbose    = FALSE )

title: Getting started with bouquets
code: cluster_bouquet(     time_col   = date,     series_col = well_id,     value_col  = gwl,     method     = "pca_hclust",     distance   = "euclidean",     resolution = 0.8   ) |>   make_plot_bouquet(     time_col      = date,     series_col    = well_id,     value_col     = gwl,     stem_colors   = cluster,     flower_colors = cluster,     facet_by      = cluster,     lon_col       = easting,     lat_col       = northing,     coord_crs     = 3035,     show_rings    = TRUE,     dark_mode     = TRUE,     title         = "Groundwater dynamics by directional cluster",     verbose       = FALSE   )

what: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series
title: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series
text: bouquets creates angular accumulation plots collections time series. series encoded turtle-graphics path turns left increases right decreases — series share origin turning angle, paths look alike come series similar directional dynamics, regardless absolute values. technique structurally related DNA walk visualisations (Gates 1986; Yau et al. 2003).

title: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series
text: Optional dependencies unlock extra features:
code: # Development version from GitHub # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("MxNl/bouquets")

title: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series
code: n      <- 52L weeks  <- seq(as.Date("2023-01-01"), by = "week", length.out = n) season <- sin(seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = n))  gw_long <- tibble::tibble(   week    = rep(weeks, 8L),   station = rep(paste0("Station ", LETTERS[1:8]), each = n),   region  = rep(c("North", "North", "North", "North",                   "South", "South", "South", "South"), each = n),   level_m = c(     8.5 + 0.8 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.00, 0.18)),     7.2 + 0.5 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.02, 0.22)),     9.1 + 1.1 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n, -0.01, 0.15)),     6.8 + 0.9 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.01, 0.20)),     5.4 + 1.2 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n, -0.02, 0.17)),     7.7 + 0.6 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.00, 0.21)),     8.9 + 0.7 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.01, 0.19)),     6.2 + 1.0 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n, -0.01, 0.16))   ) )  make_plot_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col   = week,   series_col = station,   value_col  = level_m,   verbose    = FALSE )

title: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series
code: make_plot_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col      = week,   series_col    = station,   value_col     = level_m,   stem_colors   = "greens",   flower_colors = "blossom",   verbose       = FALSE )

title: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series
text: Pass bare column name colour series grouping variable, split facets facet_by:
code: make_plot_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col      = week,   series_col    = station,   value_col     = level_m,   stem_colors   = region,   flower_colors = region,   facet_by      = region,   title         = "Groundwater dynamics by region",   verbose       = FALSE )

what: Dark mode with rings and step markers
title: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series
code: make_plot_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col      = week,   series_col    = station,   value_col     = level_m,   stem_colors   = "greens",   flower_colors = "blossom",   show_rings    = TRUE,   marker_every  = 13L,   dark_mode     = TRUE,   title         = "Groundwater — dark mode",   verbose       = FALSE )

title: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series
text: cluster_bouquet() groups series similarity directional sequences appends cluster column plugs directly make_plot_bouquet():
code: set.seed(7) gw_long |>   cluster_bouquet(     time_col   = week,     series_col = station,     value_col  = level_m,     verbose    = FALSE   ) |>   make_plot_bouquet(     time_col      = week,     series_col    = station,     value_col     = level_m,     stem_colors   = cluster,     flower_colors = cluster,     facet_by      = cluster,     title         = "Series grouped by directional dynamics",     verbose       = FALSE   )

title: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series
text: Supply coordinate columns attach location map alongside bouquet. projected CRS handled via coord_crs (e.g. coord_crs = 3035 ETRS89-LAEA).
code: # Stations spread across Germany north to south, coast to border gw_coords <- dplyr::mutate(gw_long,   lon = rep(c(6.9, 13.4, 9.9, 12.1, 7.5, 11.2, 8.7, 14.8), each = n),   lat = rep(c(53.6, 52.5, 51.5, 48.1, 51.2, 49.8, 47.8, 50.9), each = n) )  make_plot_bouquet(gw_coords,   time_col   = week,   series_col = station,   value_col  = level_m,   lon_col    = lon,   lat_col    = lat,   map_width  = 0.38,   verbose    = FALSE )

title: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series
text: series, signed first difference binarised +1 (increase), -1 (decrease), 0 (change). heading step accumulates : h_i = h_launch + sum(d_k x theta) k = 2..angle theta derived even volatile series dataset never completes full loop: theta = 360 / max_over_series(max(C) - min(C)) x ceiling_pct C cumulative sum signed steps series. coordinates follow vectorised cumsum(cos(heading)) / cumsum(sin(heading)).

title: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series
text: use bouquets publication, please cite :
code: Last, F. (2025). bouquets: Angular accumulation plots for time series. R package version 0.1.0. https://github.com/MxNl/bouquets

title: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series
text: Gates, M. . (1986). simple way look DNA. Journal Theoretical Biology, 119(3), 319-328. Yau, S. S.-T., et al. (2003). DNA sequence representation without degeneracy. Nucleic Acids Research, 31(12), 3078-3080.

what: Cluster Time Series by Directional Dynamics — cluster_bouquet
title: Cluster Time Series by Directional Dynamics — cluster_bouquet
text: Encodes time series sequence signed directional steps (\(+1\) increase, \(-1\) decrease, \(0\) change) clusters series based similarity sequences. resulting cluster column can passed directly make_plot_bouquet() via stem_colors, flower_colors, facet_by.

title: Cluster Time Series by Directional Dynamics — cluster_bouquet
code: cluster_bouquet(   data,   time_col = 1,   series_col = 2,   value_col = 3,   k = "auto",   k_max = 8L,   method = c("pca_hclust", "pca_kmeans", "hclust", "kmeans", "pam"),   distance = c("euclidean", "correlation", "manhattan"),   resolution = 0.5,   pca_variance = 0.9,   cluster_col = "cluster",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Cluster Time Series by Directional Dynamics — cluster_bouquet
text: data long-format data frame tibble — format expected make_plot_bouquet(). time_col <tidy-select> time index column. Defaults first column. series_col <tidy-select> column identifying individual time series. Defaults second column. value_col <tidy-select> numeric value column. Defaults third column. k Either positive integer specifying number clusters, "auto" (default) select optimal number maximising composite silhouette score across k = 2, …, k_max. k_max single positive integer. upper bound considered k = "auto". Capped internally n_series - 1. Defaults 8L. method clustering algorithm. One : "pca_hclust" (Default) Projects direction sequences onto principal components retaining pca_variance variance, applies agglomerative hierarchical clustering Ward's D2 linkage space. PCA step removes noise dimensions dilute cluster separation long series. Deterministic; extra packages required. distance argument apply — distance always Euclidean PCA space. "pca_kmeans" PCA projection followed k-means. Faster large numbers series; non-deterministic (set seed reproducibility). distance argument apply. "hclust" Hierarchical clustering Ward's D2 directly direction sequences, using distance metric. Best short series (< ~50 steps) raw sequence distance meaningful. "kmeans" k-means directly direction sequences. distance argument apply (k-means always uses Euclidean internally). Suitable short series many observations. "pam" Partitioning Around Medoids using distance metric. robust outlier series k-means cluster centres always real observations. Requires cluster package. distance distance metric applied raw direction sequences. One : "euclidean" (Default) Penalises step--step disagreements sequences. "correlation" Converts Pearson correlation distance \(1 - r\), measuring whether series co-move ups downs regardless absolute patterns. Often meaningful metric hydrological series. "manhattan" Sum absolute step differences. Less sensitive occasional large divergences Euclidean. Note: applied "hclust" "pam". PCA methods always use Euclidean distance reduced space; k-means distance argument design. resolution non-negative number controlling preference vs fewer clusters automatic k selection. composite score : $$\text{score}(k) = \bar{s}(k) \times \min_c \bar{s}_c(k) \times   k^{\text{resolution}}$$ \(\bar{s}(k)\) overall mean silhouette width \(\min_c \bar{s}_c(k)\) mean silhouette worst cluster. Increasing resolution rewards finer groupings long clusters remain well-defined. resolution = 0 falls back plain mean silhouette. Defaults 0.5. pca_variance number (0, 1]. cumulative proportion variance retain PCA compression. used "pca_hclust" "pca_kmeans". Defaults 0.90. cluster_col string. name column added data. Defaults "cluster". verbose Logical. Whether print k selection table, chosen k, mean silhouette, cluster sizes. Defaults TRUE.

title: Cluster Time Series by Directional Dynamics — cluster_bouquet
text: original data tibble one additional factor column named cluster_col. Levels "C1", "C2", … ordered decreasing cluster size.

title: Cluster Time Series by Directional Dynamics — cluster_bouquet
text: series length \(n\) represented vector \(n - 1\) signed steps \(d_i \\{-1, 0, +1\}\). form rows feature matrix \(M \\mathbb{R}^{n_\text{series} \times (n-1)}\).

what: Which method and distance to choose
title: Cluster Time Series by Directional Dynamics — cluster_bouquet
text: typical hydrological series (weekly daily, many years): Start "pca_hclust" (default). PCA compression removes noise makes long raw sequences hard separate. suspect series differ mainly co-movement rather exact pattern, try method = "hclust", distance = "correlation". often geologically meaningful choice. Use "pam" want cluster representatives real observed series can point data.

title: Cluster Time Series by Directional Dynamics — cluster_bouquet
text: Plain mean silhouette consistently selects k = 2 two large blobs almost always score well globally even finer structure exists. composite score adds two corrections: worst-cluster term \(\min_c \bar{s}_c(k)\) kills solutions one cluster ill-defined, resolution exponent \(k^\text{resolution}\) rewards cleaner solutions higher k.

title: Cluster Time Series by Directional Dynamics — cluster_bouquet
code: data |>   cluster_bouquet(time_col = date, series_col = id, value_col = gwl) |>   make_plot_bouquet(     time_col      = date,     series_col    = id,     value_col     = gwl,     stem_colors   = cluster,     flower_colors = cluster,     facet_by      = cluster   )

title: Cluster Time Series by Directional Dynamics — cluster_bouquet
code: set.seed(42) n      <- 52L weeks  <- seq(as.Date("2023-01-01"), by = "week", length.out = n) season <- sin(seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = n))  gw_long <- tibble::tibble(   week    = rep(weeks, 6L),   station = rep(paste0("S", 1:6), each = n),   level_m = c(     8.5 + 0.8 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.00, 0.2)),     8.3 + 0.7 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.01, 0.2)),     7.2 + 0.5 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.02, 0.2)),     7.0 + 0.6 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.00, 0.2)),     9.1 + 1.1 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n, -0.01, 0.2)),     9.3 + 1.0 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n, -0.02, 0.2))   ) )  # Default: PCA + hclust, auto k clustered <- cluster_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col = week, series_col = station, value_col = level_m) #> PCA: retaining 5 / 51 components (100% variance explained) #>  #> Auto k selection (composite silhouette score): #>   k = 2 : 0.0063 #>   k = 3 : 0.0011 #>   k = 4 : 0.0533 <-- selected #>   k = 5 : 0.0302 #>  #> cluster_bouquet  |  method = pca_hclust  |  distance = euclidean (unused) #> k = 4  |  mean silhouette = 0.053  |  resolution = 0.50 #> Cluster sizes: #>   C1 : 2 series #>   C2 : 2 series #>   C3 : 1 series #>   C4 : 1 series #>   # Correlation distance with hclust — often best for hydrological series clustered_corr <- cluster_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col   = week,   series_col = station,   value_col  = level_m,   method     = "hclust",   distance   = "correlation" ) #>  #> Auto k selection (composite silhouette score): #>   k = 2 : 0.0272 #>   k = 3 : 0.0040 #>   k = 4 : 0.1164 <-- selected #>   k = 5 : 0.0719 #>  #> cluster_bouquet  |  method = hclust  |  distance = correlation #> k = 4  |  mean silhouette = 0.116  |  resolution = 0.50 #> Cluster sizes: #>   C1 : 2 series #>   C2 : 2 series #>   C3 : 1 series #>   C4 : 1 series #>   # Increase resolution to push toward finer, cleaner clusters clustered_fine <- cluster_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col   = week,   series_col = station,   value_col  = level_m,   resolution = 1.5 ) #> PCA: retaining 5 / 51 components (100% variance explained) #>  #> Auto k selection (composite silhouette score): #>   k = 2 : 0.0126 #>   k = 3 : 0.0033 #>   k = 4 : 0.0533 <-- selected #>   k = 5 : 0.0302 #>  #> cluster_bouquet  |  method = pca_hclust  |  distance = euclidean (unused) #> k = 4  |  mean silhouette = 0.053  |  resolution = 1.50 #> Cluster sizes: #>   C1 : 2 series #>   C2 : 2 series #>   C3 : 1 series #>   C4 : 1 series #>   # Visualise — colour and facet by cluster # \donttest{ make_plot_bouquet(clustered,   time_col      = week,   series_col    = station,   value_col     = level_m,   stem_colors   = cluster,   flower_colors = cluster,   facet_by      = cluster,   title         = "Groundwater Stations by Directional Cluster" ) #> === Heading sweep per series (max - min of cumulative net steps) === #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   bq_s  cum_net_range #>   <chr>         <int> #> 1 S1               11 #> 2 S2               10 #> 3 S3               18 #> 4 S4                8 #> 5 S5               11 #> 6 S6               14 #>  #> Binding series : S3 (sweep = 18 steps) #> Ceiling angle  : 20.00° #> theta (80%)  : 16.00° #>   # }

what: Angular Accumulation (Flower Bouquet) Plot for Time Series — make_plot_bouquet
title: Angular Accumulation (Flower Bouquet) Plot for Time Series — make_plot_bouquet
text: Draws time series turtle-graphics path shared origin, encoding binary direction consecutive changes fixed-angle turns: increase turns heading left \(\theta\) decrease turns right \(\theta\). change continues straight ahead. series share origin angle, visual similarity paths directly reflects similarity fluctuation patterns across series. rotation angle \(\theta\) derived automatically data. binding constraint series whose cumulative heading sweeps widest arc (measured max(cumsum) - min(cumsum) signed steps). \(\theta\) set ceiling_pct largest angle cause series complete full 360° loop.

title: Angular Accumulation (Flower Bouquet) Plot for Time Series — make_plot_bouquet
code: make_plot_bouquet(   data,   time_col = 1,   series_col = 2,   value_col = 3,   stem_colors = "#3a7d2c",   flower_colors = "#f472b6",   facet_by = NULL,   ceiling_pct = 0.8,   launch_deg = 90,   marker_every = NULL,   show_rings = FALSE,   dark_mode = FALSE,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   hide_legend_after = 10L,   lon_col = NULL,   lat_col = NULL,   map_width = 0.35,   coord_crs = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Angular Accumulation (Flower Bouquet) Plot for Time Series — make_plot_bouquet
text: data long-format data frame tibble minimum time column, series identifier column, numeric value column. Additional factor character columns may referenced stem_colors, flower_colors, facet_by. time_col <tidy-select> time index column. Defaults first column. series_col <tidy-select> column identifying individual time series (e.g. station name). Defaults second column. value_col <tidy-select> numeric column containing measured values. Defaults third column. stem_colors Controls colour time series lines. Four options accepted: single hex string — lines share colour. Default: "#3a7d2c" (plant green). character vector hex strings, one per series — matched position; recycled warning short. "greens" — series receives distinct shade curated palette natural greens. bare column name referencing factor character column data — series coloured column's value. flower_colors Controls colour end--series ✿ symbols. Four options accepted: single hex string — flowers share colour. Default: "#f472b6" (warm pink). character vector hex strings, one per series — matched position; recycled warning short. "blossom" — flower assigned colour curated palette pinks, mauves, soft corals. bare column name referencing factor character column data — flower coloured column's value. facet_by <tidy-select> optional bare column name referencing factor character column data. supplied plot split facets via ggplot2::facet_wrap(), one panel per level. NULL (default) produces single panel. ceiling_pct single number (0, 1]. fraction theoretical maximum angle use \(\theta\). Lower values give visual headroom; higher values make turns pronounced. Default 0.80. launch_deg single number. initial heading series degrees, measured counter-clockwise positive x-axis. 90 points straight (north). Defaults 90. marker_every positive integer. supplied, filled circle plotted every marker_every-th step aid reading temporal progress. NULL (default) suppresses markers. show_rings Logical. Whether draw faint concentric reference rings centred origin. Defaults FALSE. dark_mode Logical. TRUE uses dark navy background; FALSE (default) uses light -white background. title string used plot title. NULL (default) = title. subtitle string used subtitle. NULL (default) auto-generates subtitle reporting \(\theta\), binding series, time range, interval. hide_legend_after single positive integer. legend suppressed number series greater equal value. Defaults 10. lon_col <tidy-select> Optional bare column name containing longitude series location (decimal degrees, WGS 84). Must supplied together lat_col. provided location map attached right bouquet plot. NULL (default) = map. lat_col <tidy-select> Optional bare column name containing latitude. See lon_col. map_width Relative width location map panel fraction total combined width. value 0.35 means map takes 35 \ horizontal space bouquet takes 65 \ lat_col provided. Defaults 0.35. coord_crs Integer NULL. EPSG code coordinate reference system used lon_col lat_col. NULL (default) function attempts auto-detect CRS coordinate magnitude. Supply explicit code (e.g. coord_crs = 3035 ETRS89-LAEA, coord_crs = 25832 ETRS89-UTM32) whenever auto-detection gives wrong results. Decimal-degree coordinates (WGS84 / EPSG:4326) require reprojection argument ignored. verbose Logical. Whether print angle diagnostics console. Defaults TRUE.

title: Angular Accumulation (Flower Bouquet) Plot for Time Series — make_plot_bouquet
text: lon_col lat_col NULL (default), ggplot2::ggplot object. coordinate columns supplied, patchwork object combining bouquet panel (left) location map (right); patchwork objects behave like ggplot objects print(), ggsave(), +.

title: Angular Accumulation (Flower Bouquet) Plot for Time Series — make_plot_bouquet
text: data must long format: one row per time step per series. Series share time index values equal length.

title: Angular Accumulation (Flower Bouquet) Plot for Time Series — make_plot_bouquet
text: series, signed first difference binarised \(+1\) (increase), \(-1\) (decrease), \(0\) (change). heading step \(\) accumulated : $$h_i = h_{\text{launch}} + \sum_{k=2}^{} d_k \cdot \theta$$ computed one vectorised pass via base::cumsum(). Cartesian coordinates follow : \(x_i = \sum_{k=2}^{} \cos(h_k)\), similarly \(y\).

title: Angular Accumulation (Flower Bouquet) Plot for Time Series — make_plot_bouquet
text: complete loop occurs heading sweeps \(\geq 360°\). sweep equals \((\max C - \min C) \cdot \theta\), \(C\) cumulative sum signed steps. angle therefore: $$\theta = \frac{360°}{\max_s(\max C_s - \min C_s)} \times   \texttt{ceiling\_pct}$$ Note max(abs(C)) correct constraint: series dips \(-5\) climbs \(+20\) sweeps 25 steps' worth angle even though neither extreme alone reaches 25.

title: Angular Accumulation (Flower Bouquet) Plot for Time Series — make_plot_bouquet
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(42) n      <- 52 weeks  <- seq(as.Date("2023-01-01"), by = "week", length.out = n) season <- sin(seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = n))  gw_long <- tibble::tibble(   week    = rep(weeks, 3),   station = rep(c("Station A", "Station B", "Station C"), each = n),   region  = rep(c("North", "North", "South"), each = n),   level_m = c(     8.5 + 0.8 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.00, 0.18)),     7.2 + 0.5 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.02, 0.22)),     9.1 + 1.1 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n, -0.01, 0.15))   ) )  # Minimal call make_plot_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col = week, series_col = station, value_col = level_m) #> === Heading sweep per series (max - min of cumulative net steps) === #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   bq_s      cum_net_range #>   <chr>             <int> #> 1 Station A            13 #> 2 Station B            10 #> 3 Station C            26 #>  #> Binding series : Station C (sweep = 26 steps) #> Ceiling angle  : 13.85° #> theta (80%)  : 11.08° #>    # Column-driven colours and faceting make_plot_bouquet(   data          = gw_long,   time_col      = week,   series_col    = station,   value_col     = level_m,   stem_colors   = region,   flower_colors = region,   facet_by      = region,   title         = "Groundwater Level Fluctuations \u2014 2023" ) #> === Heading sweep per series (max - min of cumulative net steps) === #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   bq_s      cum_net_range #>   <chr>             <int> #> 1 Station A            13 #> 2 Station B            10 #> 3 Station C            26 #>  #> Binding series : Station C (sweep = 26 steps) #> Ceiling angle  : 13.85° #> theta (80%)  : 11.08° #>   # }
